feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: An Unhealthy Obssession
entry.summary:
Author: Su1c1d4l_4l3x4nd3r

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,886Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 251

Summary: "All that we see or seem is but a dream within a dream.” You move back to Gotham after failing to make it in Metropolis and accidentally stumble onto a murder scene laced with Scarecrow’s fear gas. Shaken and paranoid from the exposure, you run right into Red Robin on patrol. Instead of being seen as a victim, your behavior makes you look guilty. Tim’s instincts kick in, and he convinces himself you’re hiding something. From there, he starts watching, digging, and following — obsessed with proving a guilt that isn’t really there. or Tim Drake being a fucking creep, like he is programmed to be. Tim Drake x Male reader.

entry.title: How to bond with bats
entry.summary:
Author: Bunnyreader7397

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,248Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 99

Summary: Funny random one-offs of day-to-day life in the Bat family household. When the house can’t hold anymore, the chaos floods into Gotham from time to time.Basically, a place to just throw a bunch of silly little things for my head-canons. Request accepted.I don't currently have a beta, so I apologize if some grammar is fucked up. If you'd be interested in partnering, let me know.

entry.title: Kryptonian Reproduction, an Observation
entry.summary:
Author: Winkaku

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 87,425Chapters: 28/28Kudos: 1,731

Summary: They'd been caught in each others orbit for some time now and, quite frankly, Clark should have seen this coming. Crazy was part and parcel to Gotham after all... let alone his own life. His father had warned him about mad scientists, but Clark had the distinct feeling that this was not what he'd envisioned in the slightest.AKA: A series of PWP vignettes wherein the plot is entirely background and I burn through kinks faster than a sorority freshman. Seriously, I’m running out of ideas, leave a comment with a suggestion and I’ll see what I can do.AKA: That time I tried to make a kinky science fic that turned into a writing challenge that turned into a crazy romp with associated art pieces. Fun was had by all.

entry.title: An Emerald's Gaze
entry.summary:
Author: Kyronie

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,202Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 21

Summary: "Guys are we sure this is the correct place?' Clint asked pushing his way through rock and rubble. "Guys!" Natasha called out standing over an unknown substance on the ground. When the Avengers answered the alarm about a surge in magical energy in Queens, they were expecting finding Loki or another sorcerer. Finding a freshly resurrected Spider-Man sitting in a green fluorescent puddle was not on that list. OR Peter Parker is revived using the Lazarus Pits and the Avengers have to figure out how their youngest member has returned from the dead. Also why he now looks like a green eyed skunk. Bruce Wayne wants to know why his family's tragedy was done again.

entry.title: Puzzles and Platonia
entry.summary:
Author: StarwriterOdeya

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,922Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 0

Summary: Lex Luthor received a sentient email and followed the ritual to be left alone. Clark Kent was called by Lepon naapurusto to meet the Honoured Spirit. He hadn't heard of the planet it belonged to because it had been fictional. He greeted Faith Lawson, Ascended name Odeya. Their challenges were easily conquered, including the one Faith thought would be the most troublesome. The Coarse-Born reverted to Metropolites and went their ways. Clark returned to work and sent The Flash, Barry Allen, to keep Faith company and bring lunch. Flash and Superman thought that Faith should stay, given her knowledge and abilities, and Flash helped Faith choose her hero name, Aileni. He brought her to Gotham, having seen her phone case and wallet, and they found a great option in the listings Batman provided, Atrium Residencies. Faith had powerful Forms and Modes thanks to her Role in Thalassic Space and the Ever-Light Cloth, but had physical disabilities because of her dwarfism and was mortal. She could see Hidden Object Scenes and puzzles, and had good eyesight, resilience, and charm. But she wrote fantasy-horror, had a few phobias, and randomly faced Master difficulty logic puzzles to make things "fair". Nothing could go wrong!

entry.title: Two Heroes and a Baby
entry.summary:
Author: xxEmilee_Lunaxx

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 125,813Chapters: 35/?Kudos: 629

Summary: In this AU, 19-year-old Bruce Wayne adopts 4-year-old Dick Grayson after he is orphaned by a tragic event at Haly's Circus. Bruce is in over his head trying to juggle establishing himself as a responsible and valuable asset to Wayne Enterprises, beginning his vigilante work in Gotham as The Batman, and parenting a young child despite not knowing where to start. After struggling to get good press and constantly being seen as an irresponsible nepo baby, he sets up an interview at The Daily Planet. That's where he meets 19-year-old intern Clark Kent, who is eager to write a good story to begin his career as a journalist. After Clark agrees to keep an eye on Dick during his interview, Bruce realizes he has a knack for watching children, especially young Dick Grayson. Bruce asks Clark to come live with him at Wayne Manor on a 6 month assignment as Dick's nanny, and in return, will let Clark write the story of a lifetime--an expose on his life that no other reporter has even come close to. Clark reluctantly agrees, but is hiding a secret from Bruce--his life as the superhero, Superman! As the two begin their friendship and double lives as heroes, they learn valuable lessons in friendship, love, and parenting.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Bats Are Social Creatures
entry.summary:
Author: ope-uh-oh (shortjacketlongskirt)

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 26,274Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 1,328

Summary: Batman never makes time to socialize, so it’s been hard for the team to get to know him, and even harder to guess his secret identity. Clark is hopeful when Batman finally agrees to join them on an after-hours outing.

entry.title: The Detective’s Greatest Blindspot
entry.summary:
Author: Tigerphoenix

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,252Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 58

Summary: While he just wants Superman to stay on his side of the bay, Bruce encounters reporter Clark Kent at a gala, who calls Bruce out on his "Brucie" act. What starts as a tense "what are you hiding?" case turns into a genuine connection, where Bruce is so convinced that Clark Kent and the alien savior of Metropolis could never be the same person, he misses Clark's biggest secret. Then disaster strikes. In a Superman fight, the Daily Planet is destroyed, and witnesses say Clark was inside. Clark is dead—Superman's negligence killed him—and it has turned the Bat into a hunter, looking for a way to make the god pay for killing the best man he had ever known.

entry.title: a glowing blast for your thoughts
entry.summary:
Author: Ana Luz (AnaLuz13)

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 22,693Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 43

Summary: After a battle and being shot with a magical ray, Batman, Bruce Wayne, starts to hear other people’s thoughts. Actually, he starts to hear only one particular alien’s thoughts. He plans to tell him and fix the situation, until Bruce listens to very interesting and private intentions of what said alien wants to do with him and to his body.

entry.title: Dead Reckoning Part Two
entry.summary:
Author: ChemShaw

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 175,587Chapters: 10/12Kudos: 10

Summary: Aven is living on borrowed time, his body harboring something intelligent, dangerous, and very much awake. As powerful corporations circle and a desperate mission pushes him deep into enemy territory, survival demands disguises, secrets, and impossible trust. Love offers brief sanctuary, even as it makes everything more fragile. Hunted, exposed, and running out of room to run, Aven must face the truth:staying alive may cost more than dying ever would...

entry.title: Parent Trap
entry.summary:
Author: i8thecookie

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 11,319Chapters: 4/7Kudos: 210

Summary: When Dick Grayson forces Clark to pretend to be his parent for a meeting with his headmaster, Clark expects an awkward conversation and maybe a lecture from Bruce afterward. He does not expect Gotham’s tabloids to decide he and Bruce Wayne are secretly together. But after one too many headlines about Bruce Wayne’s “mysterious co-parent,” pretending to date starts seeming like the easiest solution. That would probably be less of a problem if Clark wasn’t already half in love with him.

entry.summary:
Author: pommebeerbomb

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,375Chapters: 3/15Kudos: 43

Summary: Bruce and Clark are dormmates in MetU, but of course Clark is unaware of that. Clark's mysterious roommate has his name redacted in the lease papers and they've never ran into each other in their dormitory where they are both awake. Could their resolve go past a series of sticky notes exchange?

entry.title: Investment Opportunities
entry.summary:
Author: bitochondria

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 40,742Chapters: 9/9Kudos: 25

Summary: Although Clark swore off Bruce Wayne after their awkward-but-revelatory tryst at the charity gala a few months back, Clark is also a nice man who was raised right-- so, since he keeps crossing paths with Batman anyway, he tries his best to be the bigger person. Even if Bruce is kind of a jerk. And also really hot. But also still a jerk. When a case involves a Metropolis politician and a Gotham pharmaceutical corporation, however, he finds himself in much closer contact with Bruce than he intended or expected, and discovers he doesn't really hate it, either.

entry.title: A brief story of how Tim Drake was outed as a pervert, and eventually cleared his name, to a certain extent.
entry.summary:
Author: CertifiedNightwingLover

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,451Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 13

Summary: One thing leads to another and Tim ends up with a Batman X Superman fanfiction on his screen. Everything's fine, until it isn't. That being, Bruce walks in on him reading said fanfiction. Bruce's interest is piqued and he reads the fanfiction, for safety reasons. Now, Bruce probably thinks Tim is a pervert, given that he can't even stare him in the eye. And Bruce...he has a lot on his mind.

entry.title: The Gravity of Small Things
entry.summary:
Author: Yevgeny

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 68,905Chapters: 12/?Kudos: 89

Summary: Clark Kent has spent his entire college career becoming impossible to notice. Bruce Wayne has spent his becoming impossible to know. By all reasonable calculations, they should have stayed strangers—two people on a campus of twenty-eight thousand, each too committed to their own disappearing act to stumble into anyone else's. Reasonable calculations, as it turns out, do not account for assigned seating, a shared diner booth at eleven p.m., or the oldest rule in physics: that two things with enough mass, brought close enough together, will always—eventually, without fail—begin to pull.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Kryptomnesia
entry.summary:
Author: superguyfan

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 25,123Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 27

Summary: “Uh hi! Apologies for the intrusion, I’m Clark Kent with the Daily Planet, I just wanted to know if you might give me a quote on the van that attempted to run you and your colleagues over.” “Looks like you pretty much got the gist of it. I gotta thank you for the school teacher cover, it’s giving me an excuse to leave right now.” Clark gave a bewildered look as if he was trying to feign ignorance. “Drop the act. I may have almost been run over but that doesn’t mean I lost my eyes and ears in the process. A school teacher calling a hospital? Really?” Clark fixed his glasses, a small chuckle escaping his lips. “It’s true what they say then, you’re pretty good.” “Not sure if I would call doing my job ‘pretty good,’ but that’s why you’re the reporter and I’m the detective. Now if you’ll excuse me...” Bruce Wayne has it all: a solid career, a family fortune, but what happens when a huge conspiracy falls right into his lap and he suddenly finds himself in a load of trouble and no one but a rookie reporter to help him out?

entry.title: Bats in the Attic
entry.summary:
Author: Anonymous

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,490Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 56

Summary: Where he came from or what his motives were weren't immediately clear. He and the other Kryptonians attacked out of nowhere. At first, Bruce suspected some sort of mind control. But when Kal-El swatted Bruce's green kryptonite out of his hand like it was a child's toy, laughing and unaffected, he knew it was something deeper than that. But his contingencies failed, one after another. Bruce failed. Kal-El brought him, beaten half to death, here. Locked him in this lonely cell… and nursed him back to health. Almost tenderly. He's healed now, but no more able to escape then when he was to injured too move. He tells himself he's biding his time, waiting for an opportunity to escape. To fight back. For Kal-El to make a mistake that he can use. In the meantime, Kal-El comes to him every night.
